behaviors.description: The evaluate-stage command stores grounding into a dedicated `grounding` artifact, not the `plan` artifact.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 Integration: the failure boundary is command-to-artifact-store wiring (artifact key/slot handoff), not a pure local algorithm. A unit test on one function can miss a miswired repository/write path. TWO-PASS down-level check rejected: behavior text explicitly requires writing to one persisted artifact and not another (`grounding` vs `plan`), which is a module interaction. Up-level check rejected: no real OS/network/subprocess is required, and correctness does not depend on model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run evaluate-stage command with an in-process artifact store fixture; assert a `grounding` artifact is created/updated and `plan` is unchanged by evaluate.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: The test requires cross-module write wiring; a unit-only check is insufficient.

behaviors.description: The implement-stage flow reads grounding when available and still writes its execution output to `plan`.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 Integration: this is read/write orchestration across modules (artifact read path + implement writer). TWO-PASS down-level check rejected: single-function tests can pass while real wiring reads wrong artifact key or writes to wrong slot. Up-level check rejected: behavior focuses on data plumbing (`reads grounding`, `writes ... to plan`), not LLM judgment quality; no real OS/network/subprocess requirement is stated.
behaviors.test_description: Seed run context with a `grounding` artifact, execute implement flow with deterministic stage inputs, and assert implement consumed grounding and emitted output to `plan` without mutating grounding.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Covers module handoff and dual artifact semantics that unit tests can miss.

behaviors.description: Deep-dive no longer overwrites `plan` as a redundant side effect when diagnosis already exists in issue content.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 Integration: failure appears when deep-dive command logic interacts with artifact persistence side effects. TWO-PASS down-level check rejected: pure conditional-logic unit tests do not prove absence of actual `plan` write side effect. Up-level check rejected: no external infra or model-quality assertion is required; behavior is about suppressing a storage side effect.
behaviors.test_description: Provide issue content containing diagnosis, run deep-dive stage against an in-process artifact store, and assert no write/update to `plan` occurs.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Needs observable persistence side effects, not just branch coverage.

behaviors.description: Artifact lineage is preserved so implement can read the `grounding` artifact produced earlier in the same run context.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 Integration: lineage bugs are typically run-context and artifact-index handoff issues across modules. TWO-PASS down-level check rejected: unit tests on lineage helpers can miss end-to-end context lookup/join errors. Up-level check considered and rejected: behavior text is explicit about lineage/readability in same run context, not about LLM output correctness; can be validated with deterministic fixtures.
behaviors.test_description: Create a run context, write grounding in an earlier stage, then invoke implement lookup path and assert it resolves the same grounding artifact by lineage metadata.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Directly tests cross-stage context linkage requiring module wiring.

behaviors.description: In a full multi-step agent run (`deep-dive -> write-plan/evaluate -> implement`), the final implementation plan reflects grounding produced earlier in the run.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 Workflow: behavior explicitly requires a full multi-step agent pipeline and an end result that reflects earlier grounding across stages. This is multiple agentic steps in sequence (deep-dive/evaluate/implement) with cross-step carryover. TWO-PASS down-level check rejected by concrete text: `full multi-step agent run` and reflection in final plan disqualify single-step Agentic/Integration minima. Higher than Workflow is not defined.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the real three-stage pipeline in one run context with real model calls enabled; assert final implement plan includes/uses grounding facts generated earlier and preserved through stage transitions.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test explicitly requires sequential multi-step agent execution and cross-step semantic carryover.

behaviors.description: When grounding is absent, implement falls back safely and still emits a valid plan artifact without corrupting other stored artifacts.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 Integration: this is fallback orchestration plus artifact-store integrity across modules (missing-read path + write path + non-target artifacts untouched). TWO-PASS down-level check rejected: unit tests may validate fallback branch but miss accidental store mutations in integration wiring. Up-level check rejected: no required real OS/network/subprocess; no dependence on model output quality is stated.
behaviors.test_description: Run implement with no grounding artifact present in context; assert a valid `plan` artifact is produced, fallback path is used, and unrelated artifacts remain byte-for-byte unchanged.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Requires observing store-level side effects across multiple artifacts.
